title: Explore applying to Linear as Product Manager
description:
Linear is hiring a Senior/Staff Product Manager (remote, NA/EU). 25,000+ companies, ~$400M valuation. They're building 'the product development system for teams and agents.'

Why this is interesting:
- MC is literally a product in the same problem space (dev workflow tooling)
- Building MC demonstrates product sense, engineering, and design taste -- all listed requirements
- They want 'shameless curiosity' and people who 'question things deeply' -- that's the MC ethos
- They want prior engineering AND design experience
- The 'for teams and agents' tagline means they're thinking about AI agent workflows too

Requirements: 6+ years in software, engineering/design background, strong product taste.
Comp: Competitive salary + equity. Remote. 5 weeks PTO.

Action items:
- Prepare MC as portfolio piece / case study
- Frame the MC story: identified gap in AI agent management, built from scratch, self-evolving system
- Research Linear's product deeply -- understand their roadmap and where MC insights apply
- Consider: what would MC look like as a Linear feature?

title: Build recording -> transcribe -> extract -> dispatch pipeline
description:
End-to-end pipeline for turning recordings into structured MC data.

Steps:
1. Record (screen + audio)
2. Transcribe via Whisper (already proven with this session)
3. Extract: findings, dispatch items, quotes, pain points, ideas
4. Auto-create findings and dispatch items from extraction
5. Archive transcript + analysis

This session is proof of concept: 2hr recording yielded 8 new findings, 15+ quotes, 16 future ideas. User called this 'the next frontier.' Should work for meetings, demos, brainstorms, and work calls.

title: Prompt analytics system: track quality, patterns, and suggest improvements
description:
Build a prompt intelligence layer:
- Track prompt specificity (vague vs specific), length, model used
- Recognize user patterns ('this user tends to X')
- Suggest prompt improvements for cost savings
- Show prompt quality trends over time
- Surface which prompt styles produce best agent outcomes

User said this is 'the next big task.' Friend's question 'did it give suggestions based off the prompts?' validated the idea. Could be a tab on /cost or its own page.

title: Prepare hooks demo for FHL week at work (Fix Hack Learn)
description:
User has FHL (Fix Hack Learn) week at Microsoft. All meetings cancelled, hack on anything.

Need to prepare:
- A compelling hooks demo that shows value in work context
- Sanitized output (no MC branding visible to coworkers)
- Clear narrative: 'here's what hooks can do for your Claude Code workflow'
- Consider: what work problems are hooks best suited to solve?

User said: 'hooks are very cool, but I don't know what's a good application of it in the context of work.' This needs to be figured out BEFORE FHL week.

title: Add prompt engineering for cost reduction card on /cost page
description:
During demo, friend asked 'did it give suggestions based off the prompts?' User immediately dispatched this.

Add a new expandable section on /cost page:
- Analyze prompt patterns that drive up cost
- Identify sessions that could use Sonnet instead of Opus
- Suggest specific prompt improvements for savings
- Show estimated savings from each recommendation
- Keep it money-focused: 'the money lens'

51% of prompts classified as vague. 34 single-prompt sessions identified as Sonnet candidates ($300/mo savings).

title: Add priority hierarchy to findings: bold/important levels, not flat categories
description:
Currently findings are flat categories (principle, design, architecture, operations, pain-point). User wants a visual hierarchy where some findings are clearly more important than others.

Needed:
- Bold/highlight treatment for critical findings
- Priority levels visible in the UI
- Visual distinction between 'nice to know' and 'never forget this'
- The existing 'importance: critical' field should drive visual weight

User said: 'findings is just like hard to see what findings are better than others.' Mentioned at [00:15:18] with 'repeated frustration.'

title: Campaign debrief: toggle between initial push and follow-up runs
description:
When reviewing a campaign, user needs to switch between initial sprint agents and follow-up/polish agents.

Needed:
- A toggle/switch on campaign page: 'Initial Push' vs 'Follow-up'
- Each view shows its own wins, losses, remaining
- Agent cards clearly tagged as 'initial' vs 'polisher' vs 'retrospector'
- Separate tracking of outcomes per push

User said: 'I should be able to switch and be like, okay, this is initial push and this is a follow-up campaign debrief.' Mentioned at [00:34:19] and [00:39:38].

title: Pulsing indicator for remaining items that an agent is actively working on
description:
When an agent is actively working on a campaign remaining item, both the remaining item and the agent card should pulse/blink in sync.

Needed:
- Visual pulse on remaining items being worked on
- Sync between agent card and remaining item
- Active items float to top of remaining list
- Clear at-a-glance indication of 'this is being worked on right now'

User said: 'any agent that's working on a remaining should have some color pulse happening.' Mentioned at [00:06:31].

title: Click-to-copy agent prompt modal from agent cards
description:
Currently to get an agent's prompt, you have to type /prompt or dig through files. Should be a single click on the agent card that opens a modal with the full prompt, copy button included.

User said: 'I should be able to click into a modal to get the prompt on the agent... I want to just click, click and paste.' Mentioned at [00:08:54].

title: Auto-dispatch agents from MC browser interface (reduce terminal friction)
description:
Long-term vision: launch agent sessions from MC's browser interface instead of manually opening CLI terminals.

Current pain: user must open terminal, paste prompt, wait. With 3-5 agents, this is significant friction. Eventually MC should have a 'dispatch' button that opens a Claude Code session with the right prompt.

User said: 'maybe we can make it so... when I'm ready to be like OK yeah go dispatch it.' Mentioned at [00:22:05].

title: Work presentation mode: sanitized MC view with no branding
description:
When presenting work outputs to managers or peers, MC branding and personal context should be hidden.

Needed:
- A 'presentation mode' that strips MC branding
- Clean, professional output view
- No personal context visible (home tasks, cost data, etc.)
- Tony Stark principle: outputs look like impressive individual work

User said: 'I don't want someone to be able to infer that I had mission control.' Mentioned at [01:49:47].

title: Set up Discord community for MC knowledge sharing (2-3 OSU peers)
description:
User is starting a Discord with 2-3 peers from OSU for knowledge sharing. Share before/after screenshots, prompt techniques, and workflow tips.

User said: 'I'm making a Discord with some people from OSU... that's where I put some of these [before/after screenshots].' Already in progress as of demo session. Track it here for accountability.
